channel.title: Korea-Travel on paper.archive
channel.description: Recent content in Korea-Travel on paper.archive

item.title: Plant Shopping at Joinfolia in Paju
item.description:
We went to Joinfolia in Paju to look around and came home with a few more plants.

The place is much bigger than it looks from outside. Plants fill both sides of the aisles, with hanging varieties covering the greenhouse ceiling. It feels closer to walking through a botanical garden than visiting an ordinary plant shop.

The tillandsia section has everything from tiny air plants to specimens mounted on wood and arranged inside glass balls. There is enough variety that it is easy to lose track of time.

item.title: Baekban — A Proper Korean Set Meal in Paju
item.description:
Lunch at Bapsarang, a countryside diner in Paju (north of Seoul), the kind of place Koreans call a baekban-jip (백반집).

What is baekban? Baekban (백반) is Korea’s everyday set meal: rice, a stew, a main, and a spread of banchan (반찬, shared side dishes) — all included, no extra charge, refills welcome. It’s home cooking sold cheap.

The banchan is the soul of a baekban place: stir-fried dried shrimp, acorn jelly with quail eggs, seasoned burdock and greens, fresh kimchi. You graze across everything and suddenly your rice bowl is empty.

item.title: Geoje Panorama Cable Car — Nojasan Observatory
item.description:
We rode the Geoje Panorama Cable Car (거제 파노라마 케이블카). It runs in one go from Hakdong Pass up to the summit of Nojasan (노자산), a mountain on Geoje Island off the southern coast.

The moment you step off at the top, the ridges just keep folding into the distance. This is not a view you get around Seoul.

item.title: Hanchi Mulhoe & Octopus Rice Bowl — Yoonsikdang, Geoje
item.description:
Lunch on Geoje Island at a place called Yoonsikdang. We ordered hanchi mulhoe and a rock-octopus rice bowl.

Hanchi mulhoe

Mulhoe (물회) is one of Korea’s great summer seafood dishes: raw seafood tossed in a sweet-tangy gochujang dressing, then submerged in icy broth — basically a cold, drinkable raw-fish salad. Hanchi (한치) is a small squid prized for its tender crunch. On a hot day this is hard to beat.

item.title: Dongtae-tang Breakfast on Geoje Island
item.description:
Day two on Geoje Island. Breakfast at the pollack-stew place right in front of our lodging.

What is it? Dongtae-tang (동태탕) is a spicy stew made with frozen pollack, tofu, radish, and enoki mushrooms, served bubbling in an earthenware pot. In Korea a hot, spicy soup is a completely normal breakfast — especially the morning after drinks, when it doubles as haejang (해장, “hangover-chasing”).

The pollack flakes apart softly, and the broth is that classic combo of refreshing and spicy at once. Having a place like this next to your lodging is a small travel win.

item.title: Milmyeon on Geoje Island — Woomyeonga
item.description:
Found a great noodle place on Geoje Island (south coast of Korea) called Woomyeonga. This one was seriously good.

What is milmyeon? Milmyeon (밀면) is a chilled wheat-noodle soup from the Busan/Gyeongsang region — a southern cousin of the famous naengmyeon, but made with wheat flour instead of buckwheat, so the noodles come out bouncier and smoother. Perfect summer food.

I had the mul-milmyeon (물밀면, “water” version with icy broth). The broth is clear but surprisingly rich, topped with cucumber, sliced pork, and half a boiled egg.
